mission.title: knative-eventing:  Standardize True/False/Unknown status throughout eventing
mission.description: Standardize True/False/Unknown status throughout eventing. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative-eventing deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative-eventing version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
helm list -n knative-eventing 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative-eventing installation.

steps.title: Review knative-eventing configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative-eventing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get configmap -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative-eventing
```
**Problem**
 Standardize True/False/Unknown status throughout eventing.

steps.title: Apply the fix for  Standardize True/False/Unknown status throughout eventing
steps.description:
Rereading this thread, I think 's suggestion of a grace period in https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/2187#issuecomment-554494137 is a good compromise. The grace period doesn't violate the rules above, because the trigger does eventually progress to False if the dependency fails to be detected within the grace period.

I'm not sure how to implement this correctly. I think it would require adding a temporary condition to the trigger signaling to the future reconciler that we have started

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in " Standardize True/False/Unknown status throughout eventing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Rereading this thread, I think 's suggestion of a grace period in https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/2187#issuecomment-554494137 is a good compromise. The grace period doesn't violate the rules above, because the trigger does eventually progress to False if the dependency fails to be detected within the grace period.

I'm not sure how to implement this correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative-eventing installed or the issue environment reproducible.
